message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "infra-fit" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: Service management and install-path discipline are unimplemented in the proposal but encode hard-won anti-patterns in the existing setup
DETAIL: The skeleton prints 'Service installation not yet implemented' and config.rs writes outrunner.yml to the process CWD. The existing setup learned the opposite lessons the hard way: (a) scripts must live at a STABLE path outside any git worktree — self-hosted-oss/install.sh copies to ~/.local/share/worldarchitect-runners/ (macOS) and linux/install.sh to root-owned /opt/jleechanorg-org-runners/ (.env mode 0640 root:runner-user so the service can read the PAT but not rewrite it); the most recent commit on this branch (b2c58658cd 'install pre-job-hook to stable local path, not repo bind-mount') is this exact lesson re-learned for the colima stack. (b) LINUX_HOST_POLICY.md: systemd Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + bounded StartLimitBurst — no bash supervisor on the hot path, no respawn storms; container restart is Docker's restart=on-failure:3. (c) The EPHEMERAL trap: linux/install.sh REFUSES to install with EPHEMERAL=true because ephemeral runners exit cleanly (status 0) and on-failure never restarts them — the pool silently drains. The proposal defaults to ephemeral runners without addressing who re-provisions them. (d) Repo CLAUDE.md 'Self-Hosted Runner Changes' section is explicit: every runner change must update install.sh (source of truth), commit plist/unit templates, go through PR, and be reproducible on a fresh machine — a `cargo install gha-isolated` + CWD-written YAML flow violates all four unless the binary is version-pinned, built/installed BY install.sh, and its config templates are committed.
RECOMMENDATION: If gha-isolated proceeds: config to a stable XDG path, launchd/systemd templates committed to the repo, installation wired through the existing install.sh, and an explicit answer to 'who restarts an ephemeral runner after its clean exit'. Otherwise it will be lost on reinstall and violates the repo's runner-change policy.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.reasoning: Every factual claim verified against the doc and repo. Doc: line 148 prints 'Service installation not yet implemented'; line 248 writes outrunner.yml via bare relative path (CWD); line 10 defaults to ephemeral/Scale Sets with runner lifecycle left as placeholder; line 284 proposes unpinned cargo install. Repo: self-hosted-oss/install.sh installs to ~/.local/share/worldarchitect-runners (line 12); linux/install.sh installs to /opt/jleechanorg-org-runners (line 50) with .env 0640 root:$RUNNER_USER (lines 230-244); the EPHEMERAL=true guard exists (lines 146-166) with the exact clean-exit/pool-drain rationale, also documented in LINUX_HOST_POLICY.md lines 42-44; LINUX_HOST_POLICY.md confirms Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + StartLimitBurst=3 + docker restart=on-failure:3; commit b2c58658cd exists and is precisely the stable-path lesson cited; CLAUDE.md's 'Self-Hosted Runner Changes' policy requires install.sh integration, committed templates, and PR flow. Two immaterial nuances: linux/install.sh has an explicit --allow-ephemeral override (refuse-by-default, not absolute), and if gha-outrunner truly implements Scale Sets the listener would re-provision ephemeral runners — but the doc is silent on this, so the finding's demand for an explicit answer stands. Severity 'major' is proportionate given the doc contradicts written repo policy and a lesson re-learned in this branch's HEAD commit. The recommendation is actionable and correct.
